enums.name: BearerTokenFormat
enums.description: Controls how HTTP bearer token authentication is handled.

values.name: BEARER_TOKEN_FORMAT_DEFAULT
values.description:
The default behavior is to pass bearer tokens to upstream applications
without interpreting them.

values.name: BEARER_TOKEN_FORMAT_IDP_ACCESS_TOKEN
values.description: The bearer token will be interpreted as an IdP-issued access token.

values.name: BEARER_TOKEN_FORMAT_IDP_IDENTITY_TOKEN
values.description: The bearer token will be interpreted as an IdP-issued identity token.

enums.name: CodecType
enums.description: CodecType defines the codec type to use for connections.

values.name: UNKNOWN
values.description: The health status is not known. This is interpreted by Envoy as ``HEALTHY``.

values.name: DRAINING
values.description:
Connection draining in progress. E.g.,
`<https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/elb-connection-draining-remove-instances-from-service-with-care/>`_
or
`<https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/load-balancing/enabling-connection-draining>`_.
This is interpreted by Envoy as ``UNHEALTHY``.

values.name: TIMEOUT
values.description:
Health check timed out. This is part of HDS and is interpreted by Envoy as
``UNHEALTHY``.

values.name: IssuerHostOnly
values.description:
Issuer strings will be the hostname of the route, with no scheme or
trailing slash.

values.name: IssuerURI
values.description:
Issuer strings will be a complete URI, including the scheme and ending
with a trailing slash.

enums.name: LoadBalancingPolicy
enums.description:
LoadBalancingPolicy defines the strategy used to balance requests across
upstream endpoints

values.name: LOAD_BALANCING_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED
values.description: Default load balancing if not specified

values.name: LOAD_BALANCING_POLICY_LEAST_REQUEST
values.description: Select backend with fewest active requests

values.name: OAUTH2_AUTH_STYLE_IN_PARAMS
values.description:
OAUTH2_AUTH_STYLE_IN_PARAMS sends the "client_id" and "client_secret"
in the POST body as application/x-www-form-urlencoded parameters.

values.name: OAUTH2_AUTH_STYLE_IN_HEADER
values.description:
OAUTH2_AUTH_STYLE_IN_HEADER sends the client_id and client_password
using HTTP Basic Authorization. This is an optional style
described in the OAuth2 RFC 6749 section 2.3.1.

messages.name: CertificateInfo
messages.description:
CertificateInfo is a .proto reflection of
https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/x509/#Certificate

messages.name: CircuitBreakerThresholds
messages.description: CircuitBreakerThresholds defines CircuitBreaker settings.

fields.name: max_connections
fields.description:
The maximum number of connections that Envoy will make to the upstream
cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: max_pending_requests
fields.description:
The maximum number of pending requests that Envoy will allow to the
upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024. This limit is
applied as a connection limit for non-HTTP traffic.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: max_requests
fields.description:
The maximum number of parallel requests that Envoy will make to the
upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024. This limit does
not apply to non-HTTP traffic.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: max_retries
fields.description:
The maximum number of parallel retries that Envoy will allow to the
upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 3.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: max_connection_pools
fields.description:
The maximum number of connection pools per cluster that Envoy will
concurrently support at once. If not specified, the default is unlimited.
Set this for clusters which create a large number of connection pools.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: Config
messages.description: A bundle of Pomerium configuration including routes and settings.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the configuration.

fields.name: routes
fields.description: All of the routes in the configuration bundle.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: settings
fields.description: All of the settings for the configuration bundle.

messages.name: EntityInfo
messages.description: EntityInfo is the basic metadata for an entity.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the entity.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the entity.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the entity was last modified.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: HealthCheck
messages.description: [#next-free-field: 27]

fields.name: timeout
fields.description:
The time to wait for a health check response. If the timeout is reached the
health check attempt will be considered a failure.

fields.name: interval
fields.description: The interval between health checks.

fields.name: initial_jitter
fields.description:
An optional jitter amount in milliseconds. If specified, Envoy will start health
checking after for a random time in ms between 0 and initial_jitter. This only
applies to the first health check.

fields.name: interval_jitter
fields.description:
An optional jitter amount in milliseconds. If specified, during every
interval Envoy will add interval_jitter to the wait time.

fields.name: interval_jitter_percent
fields.description:
An optional jitter amount as a percentage of interval_ms. If specified,
during every interval Envoy will add ``interval_ms`` *
``interval_jitter_percent`` / 100 to the wait time.

If interval_jitter_ms and interval_jitter_percent are both set, both of
them will be used to increase the wait time.

fields.name: unhealthy_threshold
fields.description:
The number of unhealthy health checks required before a host is marked
unhealthy. Note that for ``http`` health checking if a host responds with a code not in
:ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>`
or :ref:`retriable_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.retriable_statuses>`,
this threshold is ignored and the host is considered immediately unhealthy.

fields.name: healthy_threshold
fields.description:
The number of healthy health checks required before a host is marked
healthy. Note that during startup, only a single successful health check is
required to mark a host healthy.

fields.name: alt_port
fields.description: [#not-implemented-hide:] Non-serving port for health checking.

fields.name: reuse_connection
fields.description: Reuse health check connection between health checks. Default is true.

fields.name: http_health_check
fields.description: HTTP health check.

fields.name: tcp_health_check
fields.description: TCP health check.

fields.name: grpc_health_check
fields.description: gRPC health check.

fields.name: no_traffic_interval
fields.description:
The "no traffic interval" is a special health check interval that is used when a cluster has
never had traffic routed to it. This lower interval allows cluster information to be kept up to
date, without sending a potentially large amount of active health checking traffic for no
reason. Once a cluster has been used for traffic routing, Envoy will shift back to using the
standard health check interval that is defined. Note that this interval takes precedence over
any other.

The default value for "no traffic interval" is 60 seconds.

fields.name: no_traffic_healthy_interval
fields.description:
The "no traffic healthy interval" is a special health check interval that
is used for hosts that are currently passing active health checking
(including new hosts) when the cluster has received no traffic.

This is useful for when we want to send frequent health checks with
``no_traffic_interval`` but then revert to lower frequency ``no_traffic_healthy_interval`` once
a host in the cluster is marked as healthy.

Once a cluster has been used for traffic routing, Envoy will shift back to using the
standard health check interval that is defined.

If no_traffic_healthy_interval is not set, it will default to the
no traffic interval and send that interval regardless of health state.

fields.name: unhealthy_interval
fields.description:
The "unhealthy interval" is a health check interval that is used for hosts that are marked as
unhealthy. As soon as the host is marked as healthy, Envoy will shift back to using the
standard health check interval that is defined.

The default value for "unhealthy interval" is the same as "interval".

fields.name: unhealthy_edge_interval
fields.description:
The "unhealthy edge interval" is a special health check interval that is used for the first
health check right after a host is marked as unhealthy. For subsequent health checks
Envoy will shift back to using either "unhealthy interval" if present or the standard health
check interval that is defined.

The default value for "unhealthy edge interval" is the same as "unhealthy interval".

fields.name: healthy_edge_interval
fields.description:
The "healthy edge interval" is a special health check interval that is used for the first
health check right after a host is marked as healthy. For subsequent health checks
Envoy will shift back to using the standard health check interval that is defined.

The default value for "healthy edge interval" is the same as the default interval.

fields.name: always_log_health_check_failures
fields.description:
If set to true, health check failure events will always be logged. If set to false, only the
initial health check failure event will be logged.
The default value is false.

fields.name: always_log_health_check_success
fields.description:
If set to true, health check success events will always be logged. If set to false, only host addition event will be logged
if it is the first successful health check, or if the healthy threshold is reached.
The default value is false.

fields.name: transport_socket_match_criteria
fields.description:
Optional key/value pairs that will be used to match a transport socket from those specified in the cluster's
:ref:`tranport socket matches <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket_matches>`.
For example, the following match criteria

.. code-block:: yaml

 transport_socket_match_criteria:
   useMTLS: true

Will match the following :ref:`cluster socket match <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.TransportSocketMatch>`

.. code-block:: yaml

 transport_socket_matches:
 - name: "useMTLS"
   match:
     useMTLS: true
   transport_socket:
     name: envoy.transport_sockets.tls
     config: { ... } # tls socket configuration

If this field is set, then for health checks it will supersede an entry of ``envoy.transport_socket`` in the
:ref:`LbEndpoint.Metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LbEndpoint.metadata>`.
This allows using different transport socket capabilities for health checking versus proxying to the
endpoint.

If the key/values pairs specified do not match any
:ref:`transport socket matches <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket_matches>`,
the cluster's :ref:`transport socket <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket>`
will be used for health check socket configuration.

messages.name: GrpcHealthCheck
messages.description:
`grpc.health.v1.Health
<https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto>`_-based
healthcheck. See `gRPC doc <https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md>`_
for details.

fields.name: service_name
fields.description:
An optional service name parameter which will be sent to gRPC service in
`grpc.health.v1.HealthCheckRequest
<https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto#L20>`_.
message. See `gRPC health-checking overview
<https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md>`_ for
more information.

fields.name: authority
fields.description:
The value of the :authority header in the gRPC health check request. If
left empty (default value), the name of the cluster this health check is
associated with will be used. The authority header can be customized for
a specific endpoint by setting the :ref:`hostname
<envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.HealthCheckConfig.hostname>`
field.

fields.name: statuses
fields.description: An order-independent set of health status.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: HttpHealthCheck
messages.description: [#next-free-field: 15]

fields.name: host
fields.description:
The value of the host header in the HTTP health check request. If
left empty (default value), the name of the cluster this health check is associated
with will be used. The host header can be customized for a specific endpoint by setting the
:ref:`hostname <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.HealthCheckConfig.hostname>` field.

fields.name: path
fields.description:
Specifies the HTTP path that will be requested during health checking. For example
``/healthcheck``.

fields.name: send
fields.description:
HTTP specific payload to be sent as the request body during health checking.
If specified, the method should support a request body (POST, PUT, PATCH, etc.).

fields.name: receive
fields.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP expected responses to match in the first ``response_buffer_size`` bytes of the response body.
If it is set, both the expected response check and status code determine the health check.
When checking the response, “fuzzy” matching is performed such that each payload block must be found,
and in the order specified, but not necessarily contiguous.

.. note::

  It is recommended to set ``response_buffer_size`` based on the total Payload size for efficiency.
  The default buffer size is 1024 bytes when it is not set.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: response_buffer_size
fields.description:
Specifies the size of response buffer in bytes that is used to Payload match.
The default value is 1024. Setting to 0 implies that the Payload will be matched against the entire response.

fields.name: request_headers_to_remove
fields.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP headers that should be removed from each request that is sent to the
health checked cluster.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: expected_statuses
fields.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP response statuses considered healthy. If provided, replaces default
200-only policy - 200 must be included explicitly as needed. Ranges follow half-open
semantics of :ref:`Int64Range <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.v3.Int64Range>`. The start and end of each
range are required. Only statuses in the range [100, 600) are allowed.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: retriable_statuses
fields.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP response statuses considered retriable. If provided, responses in this range
will count towards the configured :ref:`unhealthy_threshold <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.unhealthy_threshold>`,
but will not result in the host being considered immediately unhealthy. Ranges follow half-open semantics of
:ref:`Int64Range <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.v3.Int64Range>`. The start and end of each range are required.
Only statuses in the range [100, 600) are allowed. The :ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>`
field takes precedence for any range overlaps with this field i.e. if status code 200 is both retriable and expected, a 200 response will
be considered a successful health check. By default all responses not in
:ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>` will result in
the host being considered immediately unhealthy i.e. if status code 200 is expected and there are no configured retriable statuses, any
non-200 response will result in the host being marked unhealthy.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: codec_client_type
fields.description: Use specified application protocol for health checks.

fields.name: start
fields.description: start of the range (inclusive)

fields.name: end
fields.description: end of the range (exclusive)

messages.name: Payload
messages.description: Describes the encoding of the payload bytes in the payload.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Hex encoded payload. E.g., "000000FF".

fields.name: binary
fields.description: Binary payload.

fields.name: send
fields.description: Empty payloads imply a connect-only health check.

fields.name: receive
fields.description:
When checking the response, “fuzzy” matching is performed such that each
payload block must be found, and in the order specified, but not
necessarily contiguous.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: KeyPair
messages.description: A KeyPair represents a public / private key pair.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID of the key pair.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: namespace_id
fields.description: Namespace of the key pair.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: originator_id
fields.description: Originator of the key pair.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the key pair.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: certificate
fields.description: The key pair certificate raw bytes.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: key
fields.description: THe key pair private key raw bytes.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the key pair was created.

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the key pair was last modified.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the key pair.

fields.name: origin
fields.description: The origin of the key pair.

fields.name: certificate_info
fields.description: Info about any certificates the key pair has.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: KeyUsage
messages.description: KeyUsage specifies the usage flags set on a signed TLS certificate.

messages.name: MCP
messages.description: Configuration for an MCP route.

fields.name: server
fields.description: Indicates that this route fronts an MCP server.

fields.name: client
fields.description: Provides an application with external tokens for MCP server access.

messages.name: MCPClient
messages.description: Configuration for an MCP client route.

messages.name: MCPServer
messages.description: Configuration for an MCP server route.

fields.name: upstream_oauth2
fields.description:
Specifies that before the request reaches the MCP upstream server,
it should acquire an OAuth2 token.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: max_request_bytes
fields.description:
The maximum request body size in bytes that can be sent to the MCP
server (default: 4KiB).
fields.label: optional

fields.name: path
fields.description:
The path to append to the URL when returning the server URL in the
.mcp/routes endpoint. Defaults to "/".
fields.label: optional

fields.name: authorization_server_url
fields.description:
The issuer URL of the upstream authorization server. Used as a fallback
when PRM (RFC 9728) discovery fails, per the MCP spec's "abort or use
pre-configured values" guidance.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: Name
messages.description: Name defines the x509 identity

messages.name: OAuth2Endpoint
messages.description: Configuration for an OAuth2 server endpoint.

fields.name: auth_url
fields.description: The authorization URL.

fields.name: token_url
fields.description: The token URL.

fields.name: auth_style
fields.description:
if unset, auto-detect which authentication
style the provider wants by trying both ways and caching
the successful way for the future.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: consecutive_5xx
fields.description:
The number of consecutive server-side error responses (for HTTP traffic,
5xx responses; for TCP traffic, connection failures; for Redis, failure to
respond PONG; etc.) before a consecutive 5xx ejection occurs. Defaults to 5.

fields.name: interval
fields.description:
The time interval between ejection analysis sweeps. This can result in
both new ejections as well as hosts being returned to service. Defaults
to 10000ms or 10s.

fields.name: base_ejection_time
fields.description:
The base time that a host is ejected for. The real time is equal to the
base time multiplied by the number of times the host has been ejected and is
capped by :ref:`max_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_time>`.
Defaults to 30000ms or 30s.

fields.name: max_ejection_percent
fields.description:
The maximum % of an upstream cluster that can be ejected due to outlier detection. Defaults to 10% .
Will eject at least one host regardless of the value if :ref:`always_eject_one_host<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.always_eject_one_host>` is enabled.

fields.name: enforcing_consecutive_5xx
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
is detected through consecutive 5xx. This setting can be used to disable
ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.

fields.name: enforcing_success_rate
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
is detected through success rate statistics. This setting can be used to
disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.

fields.name: success_rate_minimum_hosts
fields.description:
The number of hosts in a cluster that must have enough request volume to
detect success rate outliers. If the number of hosts is less than this
setting, outlier detection via success rate statistics is not performed
for any host in the cluster. Defaults to 5.

fields.name: success_rate_request_volume
fields.description:
The minimum number of total requests that must be collected in one
interval (as defined by the interval duration above) to include this host
in success rate based outlier detection. If the volume is lower than this
setting, outlier detection via success rate statistics is not performed
for that host. Defaults to 100.

fields.name: success_rate_stdev_factor
fields.description:
This factor is used to determine the ejection threshold for success rate
outlier ejection. The ejection threshold is the difference between the
mean success rate, and the product of this factor and the standard
deviation of the mean success rate: mean - (stdev *
success_rate_stdev_factor). This factor is divided by a thousand to get a
double. That is, if the desired factor is 1.9, the runtime value should
be 1900. Defaults to 1900.

fields.name: consecutive_gateway_failure
fields.description:
The number of consecutive gateway failures (502, 503, 504 status codes)
before a consecutive gateway failure ejection occurs. Defaults to 5.

fields.name: enforcing_consecutive_gateway_failure
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
is detected through consecutive gateway failures. This setting can be
used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 0.

fields.name: split_external_local_origin_errors
fields.description:
Determines whether to distinguish local origin failures from external errors. If set to true
the following configuration parameters are taken into account:
:ref:`consecutive_local_origin_failure<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.consecutive_local_origin_failure>`,
:ref:`enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure>`
and
:ref:`enforcing_local_origin_success_rate<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.enforcing_local_origin_success_rate>`.
Defaults to false.

fields.name: consecutive_local_origin_failure
fields.description:
The number of consecutive locally originated failures before ejection
occurs. Defaults to 5. Parameter takes effect only when
:ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
is set to true.

fields.name: enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
is detected through consecutive locally originated failures. This setting can be
used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.
Parameter takes effect only when
:ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
is set to true.

fields.name: enforcing_local_origin_success_rate
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
is detected through success rate statistics for locally originated errors.
This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.
Parameter takes effect only when
:ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
is set to true.

fields.name: failure_percentage_threshold
fields.description:
The failure percentage to use when determining failure percentage-based outlier detection. If
the failure percentage of a given host is greater than or equal to this value, it will be
ejected. Defaults to 85.

fields.name: enforcing_failure_percentage
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through
failure percentage statistics. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up
slowly. Defaults to 0.

[#next-major-version: setting this without setting failure_percentage_threshold should be
invalid in v4.]

fields.name: enforcing_failure_percentage_local_origin
fields.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through
local-origin failure percentage statistics. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to
ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 0.

fields.name: failure_percentage_minimum_hosts
fields.description:
The minimum number of hosts in a cluster in order to perform failure percentage-based ejection.
If the total number of hosts in the cluster is less than this value, failure percentage-based
ejection will not be performed. Defaults to 5.

fields.name: failure_percentage_request_volume
fields.description:
The minimum number of total requests that must be collected in one interval (as defined by the
interval duration above) to perform failure percentage-based ejection for this host. If the
volume is lower than this setting, failure percentage-based ejection will not be performed for
this host. Defaults to 50.

fields.name: max_ejection_time
fields.description:
The maximum time that a host is ejected for. See :ref:`base_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>`
for more information. If not specified, the default value (300000ms or 300s) or
:ref:`base_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>` value is applied, whatever is larger.

fields.name: max_ejection_time_jitter
fields.description:
The maximum amount of jitter to add to the ejection time, in order to prevent
a 'thundering herd' effect where all proxies try to reconnect to host at the same time.
See :ref:`max_ejection_time_jitter<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>`
Defaults to 0s.

fields.name: successful_active_health_check_uneject_host
fields.description:
If active health checking is enabled and a host is ejected by outlier detection, a successful active health check
unejects the host by default and considers it as healthy. Unejection also clears all the outlier detection counters.
To change this default behavior set this config to ``false`` where active health checking will not uneject the host.
Defaults to true.

fields.name: always_eject_one_host
fields.description:
If enabled, at least one host is ejected regardless of the value of :ref:`max_ejection_percent<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_percent>`.
Defaults to false.

messages.name: Policy
messages.description: Configuration for a policy.

fields.name: enforced
fields.description:
An enforced policy is automatically applied to routes.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the policy was created.

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the policy was last modified.

fields.name: enforced_routes
fields.description:
Routes that are automatically enforced by this policy.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: assigned_routes
fields.description:
Info about routes with this policy assigned.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: namespace_name
fields.description: The name of the namespace for the policy.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: Route
messages.description: Configuration for a route.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The id of the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: namespace_id
fields.description: The namespace id of the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: originator_id
fields.description: The originator id of the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: stat_name
fields.description: The name used for stats telemetry with the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: description
fields.description: A human-readable description of the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: logo_url
fields.description: A URL for a logo for the route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: from
fields.description: The externally accessible URL for a proxied HTTP request.

fields.name: to
fields.description: The destination(s) of a proxied request.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: redirect
fields.description: Indicates that a route should be redirected.

fields.name: response
fields.description: Indicates that a route should return a direct response.

fields.name: load_balancing_weights
fields.description:
Optional load balancing weights for upstream servers defined in to.
If not specified, all upstream servers are assigned the same weight.
If provided, len(to) must equal len(load_balancing_weights).
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allowed_users
fields.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's ID matches one of the
given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL user criterion.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allowed_domains
fields.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's email address is for
one of the given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL domain criterion.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: allowed_idp_claims
fields.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's session/user claims
match one of the given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL claim
criterion.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: prefix
fields.description: Only match requests that have a path that begins with the given prefix.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Only match requests whose path is an exact match for the given path.

fields.name: regex
fields.description: Only match requests whose path matches the given regular expression.

fields.name: prefix_rewrite
fields.description:
Indicates that when a request is forwarded, the matched prefix (or path)
will be swapped with this value.

fields.name: regex_rewrite_pattern
fields.description: A regular expression used to rewrite the path of a forwarded request.

fields.name: regex_rewrite_substitution
fields.description:
When a regular expression pattern is set, what the matched pattern will
be replaced with.

fields.name: regex_priority_order
fields.description: Determines the ordering of routes that use regular expressions.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: cors_allow_preflight
fields.description:
Indicates that OPTIONS requests used for CORS can pass through
unauthenticated.

fields.name: allow_public_unauthenticated_access
fields.description: Grants access to a route without requiring any authentication.

fields.name: allow_any_authenticated_user
fields.description: Grants access to a route for any logged-in user.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: The per-route request timeout option. Cannot exceed global values.

fields.name: idle_timeout
fields.description:
The per-route request timeout for a connection that is idle with
no data exchange.

fields.name: allow_websockets
fields.description: Allow websocket requests. Also disables the default route timeout.

fields.name: allow_spdy
fields.description: Allow SPDY protocol upgrade requests.

fields.name: tls_skip_verify
fields.description:
Indicates that pomerium should perform no verification on upstream
TLS connections. Any certificate for any hostname will be accepted.

fields.name: tls_server_name
fields.description:
The server name indication (SNI) for upstream TLS connections. If
both tls_server_name and tls_upstream_server_name are set,
tls_upstream_server_name will take precedence.

fields.name: tls_upstream_server_name
fields.description:
The server name indication (SNI) for upstream TLS connections. If
both tls_server_name and tls_upstream_server_name are set,
tls_upstream_server_name will take precedence.

fields.name: tls_downstream_server_name
fields.description: The server name indication (SNI) for downstream TLS connections.

fields.name: tls_custom_ca
fields.description:
The root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to verify
certificates of upstream TLS connections.

fields.name: tls_custom_ca_file
fields.description:
A file containing root certificate authorities (in PEM format)
used to verify certificates of upstream TLS connections.

fields.name: tls_custom_ca_key_pair_id
fields.description:
A reference to a key pair containing root certificate authorities (in PEM
format) used to verify certificates of upstream TLS connections.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: tls_client_cert
fields.description:
The client certificate to present to upstream TLS connections for
mTLS.

fields.name: tls_client_key
fields.description:
The private key for the client certificate to present to upstream
TLS connections for mTLS.

fields.name: tls_client_cert_file
fields.description:
A file containing the client certificate to present to upstream TLS
connections for mTLS.

fields.name: tls_client_key_file
fields.description:
A file containing the private key for the client certificate to
present to upstream TLS connections for mTLS.

fields.name: tls_client_key_pair_id
fields.description:
A reference to the key pair containing the client certificate
and private key to present to upstream TLS connections for mTLS.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: tls_downstream_client_ca
fields.description:
The root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to verify
certificates for downstream TLS connections. If specified
downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
certificate.

fields.name: tls_downstream_client_ca_file
fields.description:
A file containing the root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to
verify certificates for downstream TLS connections. If specified
downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
certificate.

fields.name: tls_downstream_client_ca_key_pair_id
fields.description:
A reference to a key pair containing the root certificate authorities (in
PEM format) used to verify certificates for downstream TLS connections. If
specified downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
certificate.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: tls_upstream_allow_renegotiation
fields.description:
Controls whether server-initiated TLS renegotiation is allowed for
upstream servers.

fields.name: set_request_headers
fields.description:
Allows you to set both static and dynamic headers for upstream requests.
Static values can be useful if you want to pass along additional
information to upstream applications as headers, or to set a fixed
authentication header on the request. Dynamic values enable you to pass
ID and Access tokens from your identity provider to upstream
applications. The following dynamic header values are supported:

- ${pomerium.access_token}: OAuth access token from the identity provider.
- ${pomerium.client_cert_fingerprint}: Short-form SHA-256 fingerprint of the presented client certificate.
- ${pomerium.id_token}: OIDC ID token from the identity provider.
- ${pomerium.jwt}: The Pomerium assertion JWT for the logged-in user.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: remove_request_headers
fields.description:
Headers to remove from upstream requests. This can be useful if you want to
prevent privacy information from being passed to upstream applications.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: set_response_headers
fields.description:
Allows you to set static values for the given downstream response headers.
These headers will take precedence over the global set_response_headers.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: rewrite_response_headers
fields.description: Used to modify downstream response headers.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: preserve_host_header
fields.description:
Passes the Host (or :authority) header from the incoming request to the
upstream service, instead of the Host header from the route's to URL.

fields.name: pass_identity_headers
fields.description:
Sends identity headers to all upstream applications. Identity headers
include:

- X-Pomerium-Jwt-Assertion: The pomerium assertion JWT for the logged-in user.
- X-Pomerium-Claim-*: Each of the claim values specified by the jwt_claim_headers option.

When not set explicitly, the global pass_identity_headers option will be used.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: kubernetes_service_account_token
fields.description:
A Kubernetes service account token used to authenticate to the
Kubernetes API via Authorization: Bearer and Impersonate-User/Group
headers.

fields.name: kubernetes_service_account_token_file
fields.description: A file containing a Kubernetes service account token.

fields.name: kubernetes_service_account_token_key_pair_id
fields.description: A key pair reference for a Kubernetes service account token.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: enable_google_cloud_serverless_authentication
fields.description:
Adds an Authorization: Bearer ID_TOKEN header for Google Cloud
serverless upstream requests.

fields.name: jwt_issuer_format
fields.description:
Controls the format of the iss claim in JWTs passed to upstream
services by this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: jwt_groups_filter
fields.description:
Allowlist of group names or IDs to include in the Pomerium JWT for
this route.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: jwt_groups_filter_infer_from_ppl
fields.description:
Whether to add JWT groups from any PPL policies.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: bearer_token_format
fields.description:
Controls how authorization bearer tokens are interpreted for this
route. When unset, the global bearer_token_format option is used.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: depends_on
fields.description:
Additional hosts that participate in chained login redirects for
this route. Up to five hosts are supported.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: policies
fields.description: Structured sub-policies attached to this route.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: ppl_policies
fields.description: PPL policies attached to this route.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: policy_ids
fields.description: IDs of policies assigned to this route.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: host_rewrite
fields.description: Rewrites the Host or :authority header to a fixed value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: host_rewrite_header
fields.description:
Rewrites the Host or :authority header using the value of another
request header.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: host_path_regex_rewrite_pattern
fields.description:
A regular expression used to rewrite the Host or :authority header
from the request path.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: host_path_regex_rewrite_substitution
fields.description:
When host_path_regex_rewrite_pattern is set, what the matched
pattern will be replaced with to produce the Host header.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: idp_client_id
fields.description: Overrides the global identity provider client ID for this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: idp_client_secret
fields.description: Overrides the global identity provider client secret for this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: idp_access_token_allowed_audiences
fields.description:
Allowed audiences for validating IdP-issued access tokens for this
route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: show_error_details
fields.description:
If true, include policy explanation and remediation details in
authorization error responses.

fields.name: mcp
fields.description:
Model Context Protocol configuration for this route. Requires the
runtime_flags.mcp feature flag.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: circuit_breaker_thresholds
fields.description: Circuit breaker thresholds for upstream requests on this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: upstream_tunnel
fields.description: Configuration for reverse tunneling to upstreams for this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: outlier_detection
fields.description:
Passive health checking via outlier detection for upstream hosts on
this route.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: health_checks
fields.description: Active health checks to run against upstream hosts for this route.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: load_balancing_policy
fields.description:
The load balancing algorithm for upstream endpoints. Options:
ROUND_ROBIN, RING_HASH, LEAST_REQUEST, RANDOM, MAGLEV.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: healthy_panic_threshold
fields.description:
If the percentage of healthy upstream hosts drops below this value,
traffic will be sent to all hosts regardless of health status.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: allow_upgrades
fields.description: Allows HTTP upgrade requests to be forwarded upstream.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the route was created.

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the route was last modified.

fields.name: enforced_policies
fields.description:
Policies that are automatically enforced.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: assigned_policies
fields.description:
Info about policies assigned to this route.
Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: namespace_name
fields.description: The name of the namespace for the route.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: RouteDirectResponse
messages.description: Configuration for a route direct response.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The status code that Pomerium returns for a static HTTP response.
For example, 200.

fields.name: body
fields.description:
The response body that Pomerium returns for a static HTTP
response.

messages.name: RouteRedirect
messages.description: Configuration for a route that redirects to a new URL.

fields.name: https_redirect
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming scheme of a request will be swapped with
"https".
fields.label: optional

fields.name: scheme_redirect
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming scheme of a request will be swapped with
the given value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: host_redirect
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming host of a request will be swapped with
the given value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: port_redirect
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming port of a request will be swapped with
the given value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: path_redirect
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming path portion of a request URL will be
swapped with the given value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: prefix_rewrite
fields.description:
Indicates that the incoming matched path prefix portion of a request
URL will be swapped with the given value.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: response_code
fields.description: The response code used for the redirect. Defaults to 301.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: strip_query
fields.description:
Indicates that during redirection, the query portion of the URL will
be removed. Defaults to false.
fields.label: optional

messages.name: ServiceAccount
messages.description: A ServiceAccount represents a Pomerium service account.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID of the service account.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: namespace_id
fields.description: Namespace of the service account.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: originator_id
fields.description: Originator of the service account.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: description
fields.description: Description of the service account.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: user_id
fields.description: User ID of the service account.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When the service account expires.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the service account was created.

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the service account was last modified.

fields.name: accessed_at
fields.description: When the service account was last accessed.

messages.name: Settings
messages.description: Global Pomerium configuration settings.

fields.name: dns_failure_refresh_rate
fields.description: dns options
fields.label: optional

fields.name: mcp_allowed_client_id_domains
fields.description:
mcp_allowed_client_id_domains specifies the allowed domains for MCP client ID metadata URLs.
Supports wildcard patterns like "*.example.com".
This is REQUIRED when MCP is enabled - client metadata fetching will fail if empty.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: mcp_allowed_as_metadata_domains
fields.description:
mcp_allowed_as_metadata_domains specifies the allowed domains for upstream AS/PRM metadata URLs.
Supports wildcard patterns like "*.example.com".
This restricts which domains Pomerium will contact during upstream OAuth discovery.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: auto_apply_changesets
fields.description:
auto_apply_changesets controls whether or not changesets are automatically
applied or require an adminstrator to apply them. This setting is
currently only used in Pomerium Zero.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: allow_upgrades
fields.description: Allows HTTP upgrade requests to be forwarded upstream.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the settings were created.

fields.name: modified_at
fields.description: When the settings were last modified.

fields.name: service_account
fields.description: The updated service account.

fields.name: jwt
fields.description: A newly issued JWT for the service account.

messages.name: UpstreamOAuth2
messages.description:
Configuration for OAuth2 authorization for an upstream service behind
Pomerium.

fields.name: client_id
fields.description: The public identifier for an application.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description: The secret known only to the application and the authorization server.

fields.name: oauth2_endpoint
fields.description: The OAuth2 endpoint configuration.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: Scope to control what data the OAuth2 server returns.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: authorization_url_params
fields.description: Additional parameters to add to authorization requests.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: UpstreamTunnel
messages.description: Configuration for an upstream tunnel.

messages.name: VersionedConfig
messages.description: A Config along with versioning conditions for when it should be applied.

fields.name: config
fields.description: The Config that will be applied if the versioning conditions are met.

fields.name: conditions
fields.description: List of conditions that must hold in order to apply this config.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: feature
fields.description: Feature gate version to check, or unset to check overall version.
fields.label: optional

fields.name: at_least
fields.description: If set: specifies a minimum feature version (inclusive).
fields.label: optional

fields.name: less_than
fields.description: If set: specifies a maximum feature version (exclusive).
fields.label: optional

scalarValueTypes.notes: Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint32 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint64 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int32s.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int64s.
Always four bytes. More efficient than uint32 if values are often greater than 2^28.
Always eight bytes. More efficient than uint64 if values are often greater than 2^56.
A string must always contain UTF-8 encoded or 7-bit ASCII text.
May contain any arbitrary sequence of bytes.
